div.h1: the fmap problem with effectful functions
div.p: Given f :: a ⟶ Mb (a function that itself produces a wrapped value), applying fmap f to Ma yields M(Mb) — an unwanted nested wrapper.
A monad solves this by also providing bind, which flattens the result.

g.title: Lift a plain value into the monad (minimal wrapping, identity of bind). Also called return.
Unwrap a from Ma, apply f, flatten M(Mb) to Mb. Also called flatMap or >>=.
Nothing propagates automatically — short-circuits the chain. Just a0 gets unwrapped and f applied.
Apply f to every element, then flatten all resulting lists into one — this is concatMap.
